title: Cell culture vessel
abstract: A cell culture vessel (100) includes a wall including an inner surface defining a cell culture chamber of the vessel. A substrate (215) of non-porous material is positioned in the cell culture chamber between a first region of the cell culture chamber and a second region of the cell culture chamber. The substrate includes a plurality of microcavities (220), each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities includes a concave surface defining a well and an opening, the concave surface of each microcavity includes at least one aperture including a dimension less than or equal to about 15 microns defining a path from the well to the second region. A cell culture vessel including a substrate including a plurality of microcavities and a layer of porous material is also provided. Methods of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel are also provided.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A cell culture vessel comprising: a wall comprising inner surfaces defining a cell culture chamber of the vessel; a substrate of non-porous material positioned in the cell culture chamber between a first region of the cell culture chamber and a second region of the cell culture chamber, the substrate comprising a plurality of microcavities, each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities comprises a concave surface defining a well and an opening defining a path from the first region into the well, the concave surface of each microcavity comprises at least one aperture comprising a dimension less than or equal to about 15 microns defining a path from the well to the second region; and a first aperture extending through the wall in fluid communication with the first region. 2. The cell culture vessel of claim 1, wherein the wall comprises a port comprising gas-permeable material in fluid communication with the second region. 3. The cell culture vessel of claim 1, comprising a conduit connecting the first aperture to the second region. 4. The cell culture vessel of claim 1 , comprising a second aperture extending through the wall in fluid communication with the second region. 5. The cell culture vessel of claim 4, comprising a conduit connecting the second aperture to the second region. 6. The cell culture vessel of claim 1 or claim 2, comprising a conduit connecting the first region to the second region. 7. The cell culture vessel of claim 6, an end of the conduit leading from the first region to the second region is positioned in the first region spaced from the opening of each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 8. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of any one of claims 1-7, comprising: passing gas through the at least one aperture in the concave surface of at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities; and culturing cells in the at least one microcavity. 9. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of any one of claims 1-7, comprising: passing liquid through the first aperture; and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 10. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of any one of claims 1-7, comprising: passing liquid through the first aperture from outside the vessel into the first region; depositing at least a portion of the liquid in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities; and culturing cells in the at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities after depositing the at least a portion of the liquid in the at least one microcavity. 11. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 2, comprising passing gas into the gas-permeable filter of the port, and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 12. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 3, comprising passing liquid through the conduit connecting the first aperture to the second region, and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 13. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 4, comprising: passing liquid through the second aperture; and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 14. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 5, comprising: passing liquid through the conduit connecting the second aperture to the second region; and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 15. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 6 or claim 7, comprising passing gas through the conduit connecting the first region to the second region, and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 16. A cell culture vessel comprising: a wall comprising an inner surface defining a cell culture chamber of the vessel; a substrate positioned in the cell culture chamber between a first region of the cell culture chamber and a second region of the cell culture chamber, the substrate comprising a plurality of microcavities and a layer of porous material, each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities comprises a concave surface defining a well and an opening defining a path from the first region into the well, a first side of the layer of porous 

 material defines at least a portion of the concave surface of each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities, and a second side of the layer of porous material faces the second region; and a first aperture extending through the wall in fluid communication with the first region. 17. The cell culture vessel of claim 16, the first side of the layer of porous material defines the entirety of the concave surface of each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 18. The cell culture vessel of claim 16 or claim 17, each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities comprises a sidewall surface extending from the opening to the concave surface, and the first side of the layer of porous material defines at least a portion of the sidewall surface of each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 19. The cell culture vessel of claim 18, the first side of the layer of porous material defines the entirety of the sidewall surface of each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 20. The cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-19, the layer of porous material defines the entirety of the substrate. 21. The cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-20, comprising a port comprising a gas-permeable filter extending through the wall in fluid communication with the second region. 22. The cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-20, comprising a conduit connecting the first aperture to the second region. 23. The cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-20, comprising a second aperture extending through the wall in fluid communication with the second region. 24. The cell culture vessel of claim 23, comprising a conduit connecting the second aperture to the second region. 25. The cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-21, comprising a conduit connecting the first region to the second region. 26. The cell culture vessel of claim 25, an end of the conduit leading from the first region to the second region is positioned in the first region spaced from the opening of each microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 27. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-26, comprising: passing gas into the at least a portion of the concave surface defined by the first side of the layer of porous material of at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities; and culturing cells in the at least one microcavity. 28. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-26, comprising: passing liquid through the first aperture; and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 29. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of any one of claims 16-26, comprising: 

 passing liquid through the first aperture from outside the vessel into the first region; depositing at least a portion of the liquid in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities; and culturing cells in the at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities after depositing the at least a portion of the liquid in the at least one microcavity. 30. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 21, comprising passing gas into the gas-permeable filter of the port, and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 31. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 22, comprising passing liquid through the conduit connecting the first aperture to the second region, and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 32. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 23, comprising: passing liquid through the second aperture; and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 33. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 24, comprising: passing liquid through the conduit connecting the second aperture to the second region; and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities. 34. A method of culturing cells in the cell culture vessel of claim 25 or claim 26, comprising passing gas through the conduit connecting the first region to the second region, and culturing cells in at least one microcavity of the plurality of microcavities.
